name: Lemon Semolina Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the zest and juice from at least 2 lemons: reserve 1 tablespoon of zest and 1/2 cup of juice. Set aside.
2. Cream together the butter, vanilla extract, sugar, and salt until smooth.
3. Add the egg, lemon zest, and lemon juice to the mixture; beat until just combined.
4. Divide the dough into two equal balls. Wrap each ball in plastic wrap and refrigerate for one hour.
5. Roll each chilled dough ball into a log measuring approximately 10 inches long and about 2 inches in diameter.
6. Preheat oven to 325°F (160°C).
7. Cut the logs into 1/4-inch slices, then dust lightly with sugar. Use the bottom of a glass to gently roll each slice until lightly coated with sugar.
8. Place the cookies on an unbuttered baking sheet and shape them into even rounds using your fingers. Refrigerate for 10 minutes or until firm.
9. Bake in the center of the oven for about 10 minutes, or until the surface shows a slight crack (a subtle texture change rather than large cracks).
10. Allow to cool slightly before removing from the baking sheets. The cookies are most tender while hot and will become chewy as they cool.
